name: crayon-testpage-setup
description: For standalone crayon mockups, create a dedicated HTML test page that links crayon.css, overrides --paper/--noise to local ./img assets, and defines the SVG #crayon filter in the HTML.

content: In crayon-style mockups, place the project banner at the top of the hero card and increase typography scale (base text, title, subtitle, labels) to keep text readable over textured paper/noise backgrounds.

title: Sans-serif font stack for crayon mockup
rationale: A sans-serif stack improves readability on textured backgrounds while keeping a playful look through rounded system fonts.

name: crayon-text-sharpness-tweak
description: To make text slightly sharper in crayon mockups, keep the style but reduce SVG displacement strength (e.g. scale from 1.5 to 0.7) and enable font smoothing/text rendering on body.

name: font-comparison-switcher
description: For UI typography evaluation, add a dedicated comparison section with multiple font stacks rendered side-by-side and buttons that set a shared CSS variable (--ui-font) via lightweight client-side JS.

content: Apply the crayonize class consistently to both primary demo cards and font-comparison cards so all sample blocks share the same wax/crayon treatment.

title: Keep text crisp by removing displacement from text containers
rationale: Applying SVG displacement to entire cards made text hard to read. Replacing it with a low-opacity noise overlay on containers preserves crayon texture while keeping typography sharp; displacement remains on decorative imagery only.

name: crayon-effects-on-surfaces-not-text
description: To keep readability high while preserving wax style, apply stronger crayon effects to page background, card outlines, and non-text overlays/pseudo-elements; keep direct text rendering unfiltered.

content: For border-only crayon effects on replaced elements, apply edge-effect pseudo-elements on wrapper containers (e.g., banner frame, input frame) instead of directly on img/input, then keep inner content crisp.
GitHub Pages deploys for this Vite client should build inside client/, pass an explicit Vite base path for repo pages, and inject VITE_WS_URL via repository variables in the workflow.
For GitHub Pages deployments with a Vite base path (e.g. /repo/), React Router BrowserRouter must set basename={import.meta.env.BASE_URL} so routes like '/' resolve under the repo prefix.
When branding WebWare, prefer a simple SVG favicon in client/public/favicon.svg and reference it from client/index.html; hand-drawn letterforms with slight rotation fit the playful visual style.
WebWare favicon uses a playful double-W mark with the first W in blue (#2563eb) and the second W in orange (#f97316).

For Vite apps deployed to GitHub Pages project paths, avoid hardcoded root asset URLs (/img, /audio). Use import.meta.env.BASE_URL in TSX and a path strategy in CSS that resolves under the built /assets directory.

name: readme-onboarding
description: Root README should include a live play link, clear multiplayer room flow, stage summaries, scoring rules, and quick local development commands in English for onboarding.

name: readme-banner
description: Root README can embed the in-repo banner image via Markdown using client/public/img/banner.png for a visual intro section.

name: installer-wizard-stage
description: Multi-step wizard stages use an array of WizardStep objects with a 'correct' action field per step, useState for currentStep tracking, and deterministic step generation via seededRandom. Button positions are randomized per step using seed+offset. Difficulty scales the number of trap/bloatware steps.

name: scoreboard-sidebar
description: Scoreboard sidebar pattern: Use a .game-layout flex wrapper with .page content left and .stage-scoreboard (260px, sticky) right. Responsive at 900px. Shows per-stage results from stageResults Map.

content: The entire frontend UI must be in English. All user-facing labels, messages, and text in client/ components should be written in English — no German strings.
